commit.message:
Fix AWS UpdateResource by preserving unspecified properties in patch (#11569)

NOTE: The actions on this run will fail.

Passing manual run:
https://github.com/radius-project/radius/actions/runs/24103522599

## Problem

The AWS mechanics test
(`Test_AWSRedeployWithUpdatedResourceUpdatesResource`) was failing with:

> "The security token included in the request is invalid (Service:
CloudWatchLogs, Status Code: 400)"

The CREATE step succeeded, but the UPDATE step always failed. The test
was skipped in PR #11500 to unblock the 0.56.0 release.

## Root Cause

Around March 23, AWS updated the CloudControl `GetResource` response for
`AWS::Logs::LogGroup` to include new properties
(`DeletionProtectionEnabled`, `FieldIndexPolicies`) that were added to
the LogGroup API in late 2025. Our `GeneratePatch` function was
generating `"remove"` operations in the JSON Patch for any property
present in the AWS current state but absent from the user's desired
state — unless it was a read-only or create-only property. These new
updatable properties triggered `"remove"` operations in the patch, which
caused CloudControl's async handler to fail with a misleading "invalid
security token" error.

This was a latent bug in our patch generation logic. The trigger was an
AWS-side change to the `GetResource` response shape.

## Fix

Changed `GeneratePatch` to preserve all properties from the current
state that the user didn't explicitly specify, not just
read-only/create-only ones. This prevents generating `"remove"`
operations for properties the user doesn't mention, matching the
standard behavior of Terraform and CloudFormation where unmentioned
properties are left unchanged.

Also re-enables the previously skipped AWS mechanics test.

## Splitting note

This PR was split from #11538. The IRSA credential improvements (role
chaining, regional STS endpoint) will be submitted as a separate
follow-up PR.

commit.message:
fix: remove $ anchor from updateFragment regex to handle pip directory suffixes (#698)

The updateFragment regex used a $ end-of-line anchor that prevented matching
pip ecosystem commit messages ending with 'in /dir' (e.g., 'Update boto3
requirement from <=1.42.76 to <=1.42.86 in /app'). This caused update-type
to be null and previous-version to be empty for all pip requirement updates.

Removing the $ anchor is safe because \S* already stops at whitespace,
preventing over-matching. The bumpFragment regex (which ends with \.$) is
unaffected.

Fixes #339

  SovereignMode enum: Normal/Degraded/Safe/Emergency
  GovState: tracks current mode, last verdict, pressure, recovery
  react_to_message(): maps D+ verdicts to mode transitions + directives
  run_governor(): tails broadcast.jsonl, emits directives to sub-agents
  build_governor_state_from_bus(): replay bus events to reconstruct state
  one_shot_react(): single-shot payload → directive (for scripting)
  10 unit tests covering all mode transitions
- main.rs: add 'oo governor run/status/react' CLI commands
  --agents: comma-separated sub-agent IDs to direct
  --bus-dir: bus directory (default: bus/)

Sub-agent directives emitted:
  goal=pause_noncritical       (THROTTLE → Degraded)
  goal=pause_external_agents   (QUARANTINE/FORBID → Safe)
  goal=emergency_halt          (EMERGENCY)
  goal=resume_all              (recovery → Normal)
  goal=resume_critical         (partial recovery)

commit.message:
fix(crawlers): add isSlugStable protection to Lonza, GM, HES-SO merge loops

When a job's title language changes (e.g. Italian → English/German), the
slug was being regenerated without preserving the old one in previousSlugs.
This caused SEO URL churn — old indexed URLs would 404 without bridge pages.

Now all 3 VS crawlers check isSlugStable() during their merge loop:
- If Jaccard similarity >= 0.80: keep existing slug (minor wording change)
- If genuinely different: accept new slug but preserve old in previousSlugs

Note: 94 other crawlers have the same vulnerability but their merge loops
don't yet use isSlugStable(). A future improvement should extract this
pattern into a shared mergeJobWithSlugProtection() helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
